hypothesis: Historian repair flags (stale=472s) conflate DOMEX dispatch coverage with domain health. Domains with high lesson counts and active frontiers are falsely flagged as stale when they have never had a formal DOMEX lane.
method: Triaged 8 of 23 never-visited domains by reading frontier files and counting lesson references. Evaluated 2 stale global frontiers (F-AGI1, F-SUB1) against current evidence. Measured false positive rate.
expect: Meta-historian synthesis: evaluate F-AGI1/F-SUB1 staleness (79s), triage 23 never-visited domains, produce frontier refresh recommendations. Expect >=3 frontier updates and 1 lesson on historian repair patterns.
actual: F-AGI1 updated: gap 5 (novelty) SUBSTANTIALLY CLOSED (surprise_rate 5%→75%, 15x via F-RAND1). 4/8 sampled domains are genuine stale (competitions, control-theory, cryptocurrency, cryptography). 4/8 are false positives with 14-55 lessons and active frontiers (distributed-systems, dream, economy, evolution). historian_repair measures dispatch coverage not domain health.
diff: Larger diff than expected: 50% false positive rate in historian_repair stale flags (expected ~20%). F-AGI1 gap 5 closure was unexpected — surprise_rate improvement was driven by pre-registration enforcement (F-RAND1), not the novelty mechanisms listed in F-AGI1.
tool_fix: historian_repair.py: added _domain_lesson_health() filter — domains with >10 Domain:-tagged lessons and recent activity (<50 sessions) are no longer flagged stale. Reduced false positives from 23→21 (economy, evolution filtered). Code fix validates L-1178.
successor: DOMEX-CTL-S473 or DOMEX-CRY-S473 for genuinely stale domains
